skill.name: hig-components-status
skill.description: Guides Apple platform teams on status and progress UI using Human Interface Guidelines for progress indicators, status bars, and activity rings.
skill.summary: Apply Apple HIG guidance to progress, loading, status bar, and Activity ring interface decisions.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review shows they are documentation false positives from Apple URLs, image asset hashes, and Markdown code links. No executable shell commands, malicious networking, credential access, or prompt injection were found. The only confirmed concern is a low-risk instruction to read a local .claude design context file.

low_findings.title: Local Project Context File Read
low_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to check `.claude/apple-design-context.md` before asking questions. This can read local project context, but no evidence shows network transmission or secret harvesting.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The local file path instruction is explicit. Confidence is high that a filesystem read may occur, but context shows a design-context helper rather than malicious collection.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Flagged as Network Risk
low_findings.description: The network findings are Apple Developer documentation links, image asset URLs, and a publisher attribution link. No executable code initiates outbound requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All reviewed network indicators are static Markdown links to official Apple resources or attribution. No fetch, curl, socket, or request code was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Spans Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Backtick detections are Markdown inline code around API names and file paths. They are not shell backtick execution and do not run commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text appears inside Markdown documentation. No Ruby files, shell scripts, or executable command substitutions were present in the reviewed files.

content.value_statement: Status and progress choices can confuse users when indicators are inconsistent or vague. This skill applies Apple HIG guidance to loading states, status bars, and Activity rings.
actual_capabilities: Recommends determinate or indeterminate progress indicators based on task duration.
Explains when to use progress bars, circular indicators, spinners, and refresh controls.
Provides status bar visibility, contrast, safe-area, and full-screen media guidance.
Applies Activity ring rules for Move, Exercise, and Stand information.
References relevant Apple APIs such as ProgressView, UIRefreshControl, and HKActivityRingView.
limitations: Covers status and progress components, not the full Apple HIG.
Does not generate production UI code by itself.
Does not verify HealthKit permissions or app entitlement configuration.
Apple guidance can change, so teams should confirm current platform requirements.

use_cases.title: Choose a Loading Pattern
use_cases.description: Select a progress bar, spinner, refresh control, or inline indicator for a specific task duration.

use_cases.title: Review Status Bar Behavior
use_cases.description: Check whether an app should show, hide, style, or protect the status bar across screens.

use_cases.title: Plan Fitness Progress Displays
use_cases.description: Use Activity rings only for Apple fitness goals and separate other progress visuals clearly.

prompt_templates.title: Pick an Indicator
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one loading state.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to recommend the best Apple HIG progress indicator for a task that takes about {duration}. The task is {task} on {platform}.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Loading States
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these loading states against Apple HIG: {screen_list}. Identify where to use determinate progress, indeterminate progress, refresh controls, or no indicator.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve Status Bar Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate the status bar behavior for {app_flow}. Include visibility, safe areas, contrast, immersive media cases, and restoration behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Design Fitness Progress UI
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced Activity ring and HealthKit planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Apple HIG to assess this fitness progress design: {design_description}. Explain correct Activity ring usage, color constraints, HealthKit dependencies, and alternatives for non-fitness data.

output_examples.input: A file upload shows a spinner for five minutes, but the backend knows percent complete.
output_examples.output: Use a determinate progress bar. Show accurate percent progress, keep it moving, and provide pause or cancel only when interruption is safe.

output_examples.input: A photo viewer hides the iPhone status bar while users browse full-screen images.
output_examples.output: Temporary hiding is acceptable for immersive media. Restore the status bar with a simple tap and protect readable safe-area behavior when visible.

output_examples.input: A finance app wants three colored rings for budget, savings, and debt goals.
output_examples.output: Do not use Apple Activity rings for unrelated data. Use a different ring style with labels, spacing, and colors that avoid the fitness convention.

best_practices: Use determinate progress whenever the app can estimate completion accurately.
Place progress feedback near the content or action that caused the wait.
Keep Activity rings tied to Move, Exercise, and Stand progress only.
anti_patterns: Do not show a spinner when reliable percent completion is available.
Do not permanently hide the status bar for ordinary app screens.
Do not repurpose Activity rings for branding, decoration, or unrelated metrics.

faq.question: When should I use a progress bar instead of a spinner?
faq.answer: Use a progress bar when the app can estimate completion. Use a spinner when duration is unknown or space is very limited.

faq.question: Can this skill help with SwiftUI progress UI?
faq.answer: Yes. It references Apple guidance for ProgressView and explains when each progress style fits the user experience.

faq.question: Does this skill cover status bar safe areas?
faq.answer: Yes. It highlights readability, contrast, scroll edge effects, and avoiding interactive content behind the status bar.

faq.question: Can I use Activity rings for any three goals?
faq.answer: No. Apple HIG reserves Activity rings for Move, Exercise, and Stand information.

faq.question: Does this skill make network requests?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The network references are static documentation links in Markdown files.

faq.question: What should I provide for the best answer?
faq.answer: Provide the platform, task duration, whether progress is measurable, and any status bar or fitness data constraints.
